speaker: SERVANT
text: He’s hearing of a cause. He will come straight.

speaker: SERVANT
text: I’ll tell him of you.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Pray you do.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I’ll know

speaker: PROVOST
text: His pleasure. Maybe he will relent. Alas,

speaker: PROVOST
text: He hath but as offended in a dream.

speaker: PROVOST
text: All sects, all ages smack of this vice, and he

speaker: PROVOST
text: To die for ’t?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Now, what’s the matter, provost?

speaker: PROVOST
text: Is it your will Claudio shall die tomorrow?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Did not I tell thee yea? Hadst thou not order?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Why dost thou ask again?

speaker: PROVOST
text: Lest I might be too rash.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Under your good correction, I have seen

speaker: PROVOST
text: When, after execution, judgment hath

speaker: PROVOST
text: Repented o’er his doom.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Go to. Let that be mine.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Do you your office, or give up your place

speaker: ANGELO
text: And you shall well be spared.

speaker: PROVOST
text: I crave your Honor’s pardon.

speaker: PROVOST
text: What shall be done, sir, with the groaning Juliet?

speaker: PROVOST
text: She’s very near her hour.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Dispose of her

speaker: ANGELO
text: To some more fitter place, and that with speed.

speaker: SERVANT
text: Here is the sister of the man condemned

speaker: SERVANT
text: Desires access to you.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Hath he a sister?

speaker: PROVOST
text: Ay, my good lord, a very virtuous maid,

speaker: PROVOST
text: And to be shortly of a sisterhood,

speaker: PROVOST
text: If not already.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Well, let her be admitted.

speaker: ANGELO
text: See you the fornicatress be removed.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Let her have needful but not lavish means.

speaker: ANGELO
text: There shall be order for ’t.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Save your Honor.

speaker: ANGELO
text:
Stay a little while.
                 
                To
                 
                Isabella
                .
               You’re welcome.

speaker: ANGELO
text: What’s your will?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I am a woeful suitor to your Honor,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Please but your Honor hear me.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Well, what’s your suit?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: There is a vice that most I do abhor,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And most desire should meet the blow of justice,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For which I would not plead, but that I must;

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For which I must not plead, but that I am

speaker: ISABELLA
text: At war ’twixt will and will not.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Well, the matter?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I have a brother is condemned to die.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I do beseech you let it be his fault

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And not my brother.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Heaven give thee moving graces.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Condemn the fault, and not the actor of it?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Why, every fault’s condemned ere it be done.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Mine were the very cipher of a function

speaker: ANGELO
text: To fine the faults whose fine stands in record

speaker: ANGELO
text: And let go by the actor.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: O just but severe law!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I had a brother, then. Heaven keep your Honor.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Give ’t not o’er so. To him again, entreat him,

speaker: LUCIO
text: Kneel down before him, hang upon his gown.

speaker: LUCIO
text: You are too cold. If you should need a pin,

speaker: LUCIO
text: You could not with more tame a tongue desire it.

speaker: LUCIO
text: To him, I say.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Must he needs die?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Maiden, no remedy.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yes, I do think that you might pardon him,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And neither heaven nor man grieve at the mercy.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I will not do ’t.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But can you if you would?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Look what I will not, that I cannot do.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But might you do ’t and do the world no wrong

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If so your heart were touched with that remorse

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As mine is to him?

speaker: ANGELO
text: He’s sentenced. ’Tis too late.

speaker: LUCIO
text: You are too cold.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Too late? Why, no. I that do speak a word

speaker: ISABELLA
text: May call it back again. Well believe this:

speaker: ISABELLA
text: No ceremony that to great ones longs,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Not the king’s crown, nor the deputed sword,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: The marshal’s truncheon, nor the judge’s robe

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Become them with one half so good a grace

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As mercy does.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If he had been as you, and you as he,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: You would have slipped like him, but he like you

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Would not have been so stern.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Pray you begone.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: I would to heaven I had your potency,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And you were Isabel. Should it then be thus?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: No. I would tell what ’twere to be a judge

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And what a prisoner.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Ay, touch him; there’s the vein.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Your brother is a forfeit of the law,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And you but waste your words.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Alas, alas!

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Why all the souls that were were forfeit once,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And He that might the vantage best have took

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Found out the remedy. How would you be

speaker: ISABELLA
text: If He which is the top of judgment should

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But judge you as you are? O, think on that,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And mercy then will breathe within your lips

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Like man new-made.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Be you content, fair maid.

speaker: ANGELO
text: It is the law, not I, condemn your brother.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Were he my kinsman, brother, or my son,

speaker: ANGELO
text: It should be thus with him. He must die tomorrow.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Tomorrow? O, that’s sudden! Spare him, spare him.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: He’s not prepared for death. Even for our kitchens

speaker: ISABELLA
text: We kill the fowl of season. Shall we serve heaven

speaker: ISABELLA
text: With less respect than we do minister

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To our gross selves? Good, good my lord, bethink you.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Who is it that hath died for this offense?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: There’s many have committed it.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Ay, well said.

speaker: ANGELO
text: The law hath not been dead, though it hath slept.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Those many had not dared to do that evil

speaker: ANGELO
text: If the first that did th’ edict infringe

speaker: ANGELO
text: Had answered for his deed. Now ’tis awake,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Takes note of what is done, and, like a prophet,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Looks in a glass that shows what future evils—

speaker: ANGELO
text: Either now, or by remissness new-conceived,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And so in progress to be hatched and born—

speaker: ANGELO
text: Are now to have no successive degrees

speaker: ANGELO
text: But, ere they live, to end.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Yet show some pity.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I show it most of all when I show justice,

speaker: ANGELO
text: For then I pity those I do not know,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Which a dismissed offense would after gall,

speaker: ANGELO
text: And do him right that, answering one foul wrong,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Lives not to act another. Be satisfied;

speaker: ANGELO
text: Your brother dies tomorrow; be content.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: So you must be the first that gives this sentence,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: And he that suffers. O, it is excellent

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To have a giant’s strength, but it is tyrannous

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To use it like a giant.

speaker: LUCIO
text: That’s well said.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Could great men thunder

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As Jove himself does, Jove would never be quiet,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: For every pelting, petty officer

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Would use his heaven for thunder,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Nothing but thunder. Merciful heaven,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Thou rather with thy sharp and sulphurous bolt

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Splits the unwedgeable and gnarlèd oak,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Than the soft myrtle. But man, proud man,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Dressed in a little brief authority,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Most ignorant of what he’s most assured,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: His glassy essence, like an angry ape

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Plays such fantastic tricks before high heaven

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As makes the angels weep, who with our spleens

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Would all themselves laugh mortal.

speaker: LUCIO
text: O, to him, to him, wench. He will relent.

speaker: LUCIO
text: He’s coming. I perceive ’t.

speaker: PROVOST
text: Pray heaven she win him.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: We cannot weigh our brother with ourself.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Great men may jest with saints; ’tis wit in them,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: But in the less, foul profanation.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Thou ’rt i’ th’ right, girl. More o’ that.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That in the captain’s but a choleric word

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Which in the soldier is flat blasphemy.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Art avised o’ that? More on ’t.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Why do you put these sayings upon me?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Because authority, though it err like others,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Hath yet a kind of medicine in itself

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That skins the vice o’ th’ top. Go to your bosom,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Knock there, and ask your heart what it doth know

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That’s like my brother’s fault. If it confess

speaker: ISABELLA
text: A natural guiltiness such as is his,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Let it not sound a thought upon your tongue

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Against my brother’s life.

speaker: ANGELO
text: She speaks, and ’tis such sense

speaker: ANGELO
text: That my sense breeds with it.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Fare you well.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Gentle my lord, turn back.

speaker: ANGELO
text: I will bethink me. Come again tomorrow.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Hark how I’ll bribe you. Good my lord, turn back.

speaker: ANGELO
text: How? Bribe me?

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ay, with such gifts that heaven shall share with you.

speaker: LUCIO
text: You had marred all else.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Not with fond sicles of the tested gold,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Or stones whose rate are either rich or poor

speaker: ISABELLA
text: As fancy values them, but with true prayers

speaker: ISABELLA
text: That shall be up at heaven and enter there

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Ere sunrise, prayers from preservèd souls,

speaker: ISABELLA
text: From fasting maids whose minds are dedicate

speaker: ISABELLA
text: To nothing temporal.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Well, come to me tomorrow.

speaker: LUCIO
text: Go to, ’tis well; away.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Heaven keep your Honor safe.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Amen.

speaker: ANGELO
text: For I am that way going to temptation

speaker: ANGELO
text: Where prayers cross.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: At what hour tomorrow

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Shall I attend your Lordship?

speaker: ANGELO
text: At any time ’fore noon.

speaker: ISABELLA
text: Save your Honor.

speaker: ANGELO
text: From thee, even from thy virtue.

speaker: ANGELO
text: What’s this? What’s this? Is this her fault or mine?

speaker: ANGELO
text: The tempter or the tempted, who sins most, ha?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Not she, nor doth she tempt; but it is I

speaker: ANGELO
text: That, lying by the violet in the sun,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Do as the carrion does, not as the flower,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Corrupt with virtuous season. Can it be

speaker: ANGELO
text: That modesty may more betray our sense

speaker: ANGELO
text: Than woman’s lightness? Having waste ground enough,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Shall we desire to raze the sanctuary

speaker: ANGELO
text: And pitch our evils there? O fie, fie, fie!

speaker: ANGELO
text: What dost thou, or what art thou, Angelo?

speaker: ANGELO
text: Dost thou desire her foully for those things

speaker: ANGELO
text: That make her good? O, let her brother live.

speaker: ANGELO
text: Thieves for their robbery have authority

speaker: ANGELO
text: When judges steal themselves. What, do I love her

speaker: ANGELO
text: That I desire to hear her speak again

speaker: ANGELO
text: And feast upon her eyes? What is ’t I dream on?

speaker: ANGELO
text: O cunning enemy that, to catch a saint,

speaker: ANGELO
text: With saints dost bait thy hook. Most dangerous

speaker: ANGELO
text: Is that temptation that doth goad us on

speaker: ANGELO
text: To sin in loving virtue. Never could the strumpet

speaker: ANGELO
text: With all her double vigor, art and nature,

speaker: ANGELO
text: Once stir my temper, but this virtuous maid

speaker: ANGELO
text: Subdues me quite. Ever till now

speaker: ANGELO
text: When men were fond, I smiled and wondered how.
